binaries.title: mrxsmb.sys - Use-after-free read of VNetRoot context in tree-connect completion (CWE-416), mitigated behind a feature flag

changed_functions.name: SmbCeCompleteVNetRootConstruction
key_changes: Added a Feature_1116195130__private_IsEnabledDeviceUsageNoInline (0x14004c884) gated pre-capture of *(arg1+0x18) at function entry (0x14002d174/0x14002d178), before any reference is dropped.

At the tail, a second Feature_1116195130__private_IsEnabledDeviceUsageNoInline check (0x14002d4d9) selects the pre-captured stack value (0x14002d4fd) when enabled, or re-reads *(arg1+0x18) (0x14002d4f7) when disabled - the disabled branch is the same operation as the unpatched code.
The mitigation is gated: with the feature disabled the patched function is behaviorally identical to the unpatched function (staged rollout).

The inline ActiveVNetRoots decrement on the error path was reorganized to call the pre-existing helper SmbCeDecrementActiveVNetRootsOnVNetRootContext (unpatched 0x140001e00 / patched 0x140001810, identical in both builds). No new helper was created; this reorganization is not the security change.
changed_functions.security_notes: Use-after-free read (CWE-416): In the unpatched code, when tree-connect construction completion yields a negative status, or when the ShareType at *(config+0xbc) is not a recognized value, the error-cleanup path calls SmbCeDereferenceVNetRootContext(rbp) at 0x14000197d, dropping a reference on the VNetRoot context. The code then computes rbp+0x18 (0x140001989) and at the tail reads *(rbp+0x18) via mov rcx,[rcx] (0x140001ace), passing it to RxPostToWorkerThread (0x140001ad6). SmbCeDereferenceVNetRootContext (0x140001c10) does not free synchronously: it decrements *(context+0x8) and, on reaching zero, posts SmbCepTearDownVNetRootContext to a worker thread (0x140001d48); the teardown/free is deferred. The tail read is a use-after-free only when the teardown worker frees the object during the short window before the read. The patched build pre-captures *(arg1+0x18) at entry under Feature_1116195130__private_IsEnabledDeviceUsageNoInline and uses the captured value at the tail; with the feature disabled it re-reads *(arg1+0x18) as before.
changed_functions.assembly_evidence:
UNPATCHED SmbCeCompleteVNetRootConstruction @ 0x140001840 (error path + tail):
0x14000197a: mov  rcx, rbp
0x14000197d: call SmbCeDereferenceVNetRootContext   ; drops a ref (async teardown on last ref)
0x140001982: mov  [rbx+0x118], r14d
0x140001989: lea  rcx, [rbp+0x18]                    ; address into possibly-freed object
0x14000198d: test r13b, r13b
0x140001990: jnz  0x140001ab7
0x140001996: jmp  0x140001ab4
0x140001ab4: xor  r14d, r14d
0x140001ab7: mov  r9, [rbx+0x110]
0x140001abe: lea  r8, [rbx+0xc8]
0x140001ac5: mov  [rbx+0x11c], r14d
0x140001acc: xor  edx, edx
0x140001ace: mov  rcx, [rcx]                         ; reads *(rbp+0x18): potential UAF read
0x140001ad6: call cs:__imp_RxPostToWorkerThread

DEFERRED FREE inside SmbCeDereferenceVNetRootContext @ 0x140001c10:
0x140001c2b: lock xadd [rcx+0x8], esi                ; decrement ref count at +0x8
0x140001c50: test esi, esi
0x140001c52: jnz  0x140001d59                          ; nonzero -> no teardown
0x140001d36: lea  r9, SmbCepTearDownVNetRootContext    ; last ref: post teardown to a worker thread
0x140001d48: call cs:__imp_RxPostToWorkerThread

PATCHED SmbCeCompleteVNetRootConstruction @ 0x14002d130:
0x14002d156: mov  [rsp+pMRxDeviceObject], 0
0x14002d16b: call Feature_1116195130__private_IsEnabledDeviceUsageNoInline
0x14002d170: test eax, eax
0x14002d172: jz   0x14002d180
0x14002d174: mov  rax, [r14+0x18]                     ; pre-capture *(arg1+0x18)
0x14002d178: mov  [rsp+pMRxDeviceObject], rax
0x14002d4c2: call SmbCeDereferenceVNetRootContext
0x14002d4d9: call Feature_1116195130__private_IsEnabledDeviceUsageNoInline
0x14002d4f3: test eax, eax
0x14002d4f5: jnz  0x14002d4fd
0x14002d4f7: mov  rcx, [r14+0x18]                     ; feature OFF: re-read (same as unpatched)
0x14002d4fb: jmp  0x14002d505
0x14002d4fd: mov  rcx, [rsp+pMRxDeviceObject]         ; feature ON: use pre-captured value
0x14002d505: call cs:__imp_RxPostToWorkerThread

security_findings.entry_point: MRxSmbCreateVNetRoot @ 0x140035ab0 - SMB tree connect (VNetRoot creation) construction completion
call_chain: SmbCeDereferenceVNetRootContext @ 0x140001c10 (drops ref; last ref posts SmbCepTearDownVNetRootContext to a worker thread)
RxPostToWorkerThread @ tail 0x140001ad6 (reads *(rbp+0x18); UAF read if teardown freed the object first)
security_findings.description: SmbCeCompleteVNetRootConstruction processes SMB tree-connect (VNetRoot) construction completion in mrxsmb.sys. When the completion status is negative, or the ShareType at *(config+0xbc) is not a recognized value (setting internal status 0xc00000cb, STATUS_INVALID_NETWORK_RESPONSE), the error-cleanup path calls SmbCeDereferenceVNetRootContext(rbp) at 0x14000197d, dropping a reference on the VNetRoot context. After the call the code computes rbp+0x18 (0x140001989) and at the tail reads *(rbp+0x18) via mov rcx,[rcx] (0x140001ace), passing the device object pointer as the first argument to RxPostToWorkerThread (0x140001ad6). SmbCeDereferenceVNetRootContext does not free synchronously: it decrements *(context+0x8) and, on reaching zero, posts SmbCepTearDownVNetRootContext to a worker thread (0x140001d48) that performs the teardown/free. The tail read is a use-after-free only when that deferred teardown frees the object before the read. The patched build pre-captures *(arg1+0x18) at entry under Feature_1116195130__private_IsEnabledDeviceUsageNoInline and uses the captured value at the tail; with the feature disabled it re-reads *(arg1+0x18), identical to the unpatched code.
security_findings.trigger_conditions: 1. Set up an SMB endpoint the victim will connect to. 2. Force the error path by returning a negative NTSTATUS for the tree connect, or a ShareType byte at *(config+0xbc) that is not a recognized value (0/1/3 are recognized). 3. Induce the victim to access the path (e.g. dir \\server\share), driving MRxSmbCreateVNetRoot then SmbCeCompleteVNetRootConstruction. 4. SmbCeDereferenceVNetRootContext must drop the last reference (*(context+0x8) reaches zero) so SmbCepTearDownVNetRootContext is posted. 5. The posted teardown must free the VNetRoot block before the tail mov rcx,[rcx] reads *(rbp+0x18); there is no in-thread reclamation window between the dereference and the read, so touching freed memory requires losing this timing race against the asynchronous teardown worker.
security_findings.primitive: Use-after-free read of one pointer field (*(VNetRoot+0x18), the mini-redirector device object pointer) passed as the first argument to RxPostToWorkerThread. Reaching freed memory requires losing a race against the deferred teardown worker; no synchronous free occurs at the read site. No controlled-write, arbitrary-read, or code-execution primitive is established from this bug.
security_findings.assembly:
UNPATCHED SmbCeCompleteVNetRootConstruction @ 0x140001840:
0x14000197a: mov  rcx, rbp
0x14000197d: call SmbCeDereferenceVNetRootContext   ; drops a ref (async teardown on last ref)
0x140001989: lea  rcx, [rbp+0x18]                    ; address into possibly-freed object
0x140001ace: mov  rcx, [rcx]                         ; reads *(rbp+0x18): potential UAF read
0x140001ad6: call cs:__imp_RxPostToWorkerThread

SmbCeDereferenceVNetRootContext @ 0x140001c10 (deferred free):
0x140001c2b: lock xadd [rcx+0x8], esi                ; decrement ref count at +0x8
0x140001c50: test esi, esi
0x140001c52: jnz  0x140001d59                          ; nonzero -> no teardown
0x140001d36: lea  r9, SmbCepTearDownVNetRootContext    ; last ref: post teardown to worker
0x140001d48: call cs:__imp_RxPostToWorkerThread

PATCHED SmbCeCompleteVNetRootConstruction @ 0x14002d130:
0x14002d16b: call Feature_1116195130__private_IsEnabledDeviceUsageNoInline
0x14002d174: mov  rax, [r14+0x18]                     ; pre-capture *(arg1+0x18)
0x14002d178: mov  [rsp+pMRxDeviceObject], rax
0x14002d4d9: call Feature_1116195130__private_IsEnabledDeviceUsageNoInline
0x14002d4f7: mov  rcx, [r14+0x18]                     ; feature OFF: re-read (same as unpatched)
0x14002d4fd: mov  rcx, [rsp+pMRxDeviceObject]         ; feature ON: use pre-captured value
0x14002d505: call cs:__imp_RxPostToWorkerThread

breakpoints.why: Function entry. rcx = arg1 = VNetRoot context pointer (kept in rbp). r8d = arg3 = completion status; negative selects the error path.
Drops a reference on the VNetRoot. Inspect *(rcx+0x8): if 1, this call drops the last reference and posts SmbCepTearDownVNetRootContext to a worker thread for asynchronous teardown/free.
Reads *(rbp+0x18) for RxPostToWorkerThread. Use !pool rbp to see whether the deferred teardown has already freed the block; if so, this read is a use-after-free.

watch: rcx at 0x140001840 = arg1 = VNetRoot context pointer (the object whose reference is dropped)
r8d at 0x140001840 = arg3 = NTSTATUS; negative selects the error path
rbp = VNetRoot context pointer throughout the function
*(rbp+0x8) = reference count decremented by SmbCeDereferenceVNetRootContext; zero triggers deferred teardown
*(rbp+0x18) = mini-redirector device object pointer; the value read at the tail and passed to RxPostToWorkerThread
*(rbp+0x1b0) = ActiveVNetRoots count decremented inline on the error path
key_offsets: 0x140001879: mov rbp, rcx - sets rbp = VNetRoot context
0x1400018c6: mov rdi, [rbp+0x18] - reads the device object pointer early on the error path (for the spinlock), before the reference is dropped
0x1400018e2: lock xadd [rbp+0x1b0], esi - inline ActiveVNetRoots decrement
0x14000197d: call SmbCeDereferenceVNetRootContext - drops a ref; last ref posts async teardown
0x140001989: lea rcx, [rbp+0x18] - address into possibly-freed object
0x140001ad6: call RxPostToWorkerThread - uses the read value as first argument
0x14002d16b: patched feature check at entry; 0x14002d174 pre-capture; 0x14002d4fd use of pre-captured value
debugging_aids.trigger_setup: 1. Set up an SMB server (or point a UNC path \\server\share at one you control) that responds to the tree connect with an error status or an unrecognized ShareType byte at *(config+0xbc). 2. From the victim, access the path (e.g. dir \\server\share) to drive MRxSmbCreateVNetRoot and SmbCeCompleteVNetRootConstruction. 3. Recognized ShareType values are 0/1/3; other values set internal status 0xc00000cb and take the error path. 4. SmbCeDereferenceVNetRootContext at 0x14000197d drops the VNetRoot reference; the last reference posts SmbCepTearDownVNetRootContext. 5. The UAF read occurs at 0x140001ace only if that deferred teardown frees the block before the read.
debugging_aids.expected_observation: Because teardown is asynchronous, the tail read at 0x140001ace normally returns a still-valid device object pointer in a single-threaded observation. Enabling Driver Verifier special pool on mrxsmb.sys is the reliable way to catch the read against a freed block if the teardown worker frees it first (DRIVER_VERIFIER_DETECTED_VIOLATION). Use !pool rbp to check whether the block has been freed.
debugging_aids.notes: VNetRoot context offsets: +0x08 reference count (decremented by SmbCeDereferenceVNetRootContext); +0x18 mini-redirector device object pointer (the UAF-read target; carries a spinlock at +0x780 and OwnerThread at +0x930); +0x1a0 associated connection object; +0x1a8 connect configuration; +0x1b0 ActiveVNetRoots count; +0x1e0 current exchange/context pointer. The patched fix is gated behind Feature_1116195130__private_IsEnabledDeviceUsageNoInline (a WIL feature-staging check); when disabled, the patched build falls back to the same *(arg1+0x18) re-read and is behaviorally identical to the unpatched build, so the fix is a staged rollout. Recognized ShareType values: 0, 1, 3.

summary: The patched build adds a mitigation for a use-after-free read (CWE-416) in mrxsmb.sys's VNetRoot construction-completion handler SmbCeCompleteVNetRootConstruction (unpatched 0x140001840, patched 0x14002d130). On the SMB tree-connect error path the handler calls SmbCeDereferenceVNetRootContext(rbp), which drops a reference and, on the last reference, posts SmbCepTearDownVNetRootContext to a worker thread for asynchronous teardown/free. The handler then reads *(rbp+0x18) (the device object pointer) and passes it to RxPostToWorkerThread; this is a use-after-free read only when the deferred teardown frees the object first (a race, not a synchronous post-free read). The mitigation pre-captures *(arg1+0x18) at entry but is gated behind the WIL feature flag Feature_1116195130__private_IsEnabledDeviceUsageNoInline, with the original re-read retained on the disabled branch (staged rollout). No controlled-write or code-execution primitive is established. Severity Medium, CWE-416.
